name: Current Affairs
description: Recent events, national and international news, and general knowledge.

subcategories.name: Public Service & Institutional Reforms
subcategories.description: Reform trends and contemporary service modernization.

questions.question: What is the minimum grade level for which a Contract Appointment shall be made
options: SGL. 07.
SGL. 08.
SGL. 10.
SGL. 12.
questions.explanation: The Post to which contract appointment shall be made must not be below SGL. 12.

questions.question: The duration of a contract appointment should be one (1) or two (2) years and renewable but must not exceed what maximum period in total
options: Two years.
Three years.
Four years.
Six years.
questions.explanation: The duration of contract appointment should be one (1) or two (2) years and renewable but not more than four (4) years in all.

questions.question: What mandatory duty must a **Contract Officer** undertake as a condition for their offer of appointment
options: Mentoring serving Officers.
Obtaining foreign exchange.
Serving in a Parastatal only.
Working only from home.
questions.explanation: The contract Officer must, as part of his duties, be responsible for mentoring serving Officers as a condition for the offer of the contract appointment.

questions.question: Where retired officers are re-engaged into career posts, on what grade level shall their appointment be made
options: The grade level immediately above their retirement level.
The grade level immediately below that on which they retired.
SGL 17 only.
GL 08 only.
questions.explanation: Where retired Officers from the Service are re-engaged into career posts, the appointment shall be on grade level immediately below that on which they retired and be eligible to maintain their last step at retirement.

questions.question: What is the maximum number of times a Trade Union/Staff Association Executive member is mandated to serve in their tenure in the Public Service
options: One term.
Two terms.
Three terms.
Four terms.
questions.explanation: Trade Unions/Staff Associations are mandated to introduce tenure limit of two (2) terms in their Constitutions.

questions.question: An Officer who resigns is liable to forfeit all claims to:
options: Their pension benefits, under the applicable service framework.
Salary only, within established administrative procedures.
Vacation leave and any vacation leave or passage privileges granted.
Health Insurance, subject to existing regulatory standards.
questions.explanation: An Officer who resigns will be liable to forfeit all claims to vacation leave and any vacation leave or passage privileges granted.

questions.question: In the public service context, which option correctly addresses: The **PSR** must be reviewed how frequently to reflect realities and align with global best practices?
options: Every three years.
After every election cycle.
Every five years.
Annually.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Every five years.). The Public Service Rules (PSR) must be reviewed every five years to update the provisions of the rules.

questions.question: What process replaces the Annual Performance Evaluation Report (APER) for the Federal Public Service
options: IPPIS.
The Performance Management System (PMS).
The Scheme of Service.
Staff Inspection.
questions.explanation: The Performance Management System (PMS) shall replace the Annual Performance Evaluation Report (APER) for the Federal Public Service.

questions.question: What is the primary rationale for implementing virtual meetings/engagements in the Public Service
options: To reduce travel costs only, under the applicable service framework.
To enhance political activities, within established administrative procedures.
To respond to work demands remotely and ensure continuity during pandemics/emergencies.
To eliminate the need for physical offices.
questions.explanation: The rationale is to ensure that employees are able to respond to work demands remotely, such that MDAs continue to function adequately with minimal disruption in the face of any pandemic/emergencies.

questions.question: The Confirmation Examination Board is chaired by whom
options: Director (Promotion) FCSC.
Director (HRM) Federal Ministry of Justice.
Permanent Secretary, CMO (OHCSF).
Permanent Secretary, Federal Civil Service Commission.
questions.explanation: The Examination Board shall be responsible for the conduct and supervision of the examinations. The Board shall comprise the Permanent Secretary, CMO (OHCSF) as Chairman.

questions.question: How long after first appointment must officers eligible for compulsory confirmation examination have served before taking the exam
options: Three months.
Six months.
One year.
Two years.
questions.explanation: Newly recruited Officer who has spent six months and above shall be allowed to sit for compulsory examination for confirmation in the Service.

questions.question: What is the consequence for an officer who fails the compulsory confirmation examination after three consecutive attempts
options: Promotion deferment.
Required to resign or withdraw from the service.
Transfer to a Parastatal.
Granting of paid leave.
questions.explanation: An Officer who fails the examination after three consecutive attempts shall by virtue of such failures be required to resign or withdraw from the service.

questions.question: What category of appointments were introduced to fill observed gaps and/or shore up the capacities of existing Officers, specifically covering Contract, Internship, and Volunteerism
options: Trainee Appointments.
Non-Pensionable Appointments.
Short Term Appointments.
Ad-hoc Appointments.
questions.explanation: Appointments may be made for a prescribed period of time in the following categories: Contract Appointments, Sabbatical Appointment, Career Exchange Programme, Talent Sourcing Strategy, Internship and Volunteerism in the Federal Service.

questions.question: What is the **PSR**’s definition of **Internship**'
options: A temporary assignment abroad for serving officers under exchange arrangements.
A non-structured, unpaid role with no linkage to defined learning outcomes.
A structured learning placement tied to a student's discipline or career goal.
A post-retirement placement limited to former officers in advisory units.
questions.explanation: Internship connotes a structured experience that relates to a student’s/graduate’s discipline or career goal and helps to enhance academic, career, professional and personal development.

questions.question: What is the maximum continuous service period for which a Secondment may be granted to the service of another approved body or international organization
options: Two years (in the first instance).
Three years.
Five years.
Six years.
questions.explanation: Secondment means the temporary release of an Officer to the service of another government approved body or any recognized International Organization or body for a **specified period**.

questions.question: What grade level must an officer be on to be eligible for Sabbatical Appointment
options: GL 12 and above.
GL 14 and above.
GL 15 or equivalent and above.
GL 08 and above.
questions.explanation: Sabbatical Appointment applies to the Service is the appointment of an officer on GL 15 or equivalent and above.

questions.question: What is the consequence of making a false statement on the employment application, if detected after engagement
options: Withholding of increment, under the applicable service framework.
Transfer to a new post, within established administrative procedures.
Regarded as sufficient grounds for non-employment or for subsequent termination of appointment without notice.
Reprimand, subject to existing regulatory standards.
questions.explanation: Concealment of any of the facts.. or any false statement.. shall be regarded as sufficient grounds for non-employment or for subsequent termination of appointment without notice.

questions.question: What is the primary objective of issuing a **Certificate of Service** to an officer leaving the service
options: To grant retirement benefits, under the applicable service framework.
To serve as a reference covering the holder's public service when seeking other employment.
To exempt the officer from tax, in line with formal public service practice.
To authorize paid leave, subject to existing regulatory standards.
questions.explanation: The main purpose of issuing a Certificate of Service is that, it may be used as a reference, covering the holder's public service when seeking other employment.

questions.question: What must an officer submit upon return from annual leave to their Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office
options: Medical report, under the applicable service framework.
Progress report, within established administrative procedures.
Resumption of Duty Certificate (Form L.10).
A letter of thanks, subject to existing regulatory standards.
questions.explanation: On return from annual leave, every Officer must complete and submit a Resumption of Duty Certificate (Form L.10).

questions.question: In the context of the **PSR**, what does the term 'Junior Posts' refer to
options: Posts attracting emoluments on Grade Level 07 and above.
Posts attracting emoluments on Grade Level 06 and below.
Posts for temporary staff only.
Posts in Parastatals only.
questions.explanation: Junior Posts are posts in Ministries/Extra-Ministerial Offices attracting emoluments on Grade Level 06 and below.

questions.question: What is the entitlement for **Annual Leave** for officers on **GL 07** and above
options: 14 working days.
21 working days.
30 working days.
45 calendar days.
questions.explanation: Annual Leave entitlement for GL 07 and above is 30 working days.

questions.question: What action is required before any officer can withdraw their public record, such as personal files
options: Verbal permission from the Permanent Secretary, under the applicable service framework.
Permission from the Permanent Secretary, Service Policies and Strategies Office, OHCSF.
A police report, in line with formal public service practice.
Approval from the FCSC, subject to existing regulatory standards.
questions.explanation: No Officer may leave the Public Service take with him any public record without the written permission of the Permanent Secretary, Service Policies and Strategies Office, OHCSF.

questions.question: In the public service context, responsibility for must approve the deployment of staff in the Nigerian Civil Service belongs to which role?
options: The Minister of Communications.
The Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF).
The Federal Civil Service Commission.
The National Assembly.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (The Federal Civil Service Commission.). Appointments to offices in the Federal Civil Service are made on the authority of the Federal Civil Service Commission. Permanent Secretaries/Heads of Extra-Ministerial Offices are approve to appoint candidates where powers have been delegated.

questions.question: The **PSR** permits officers to publish or speak in public or broadcast on any matter which may reasonably be regarded as:
options: A political or administrative nature, without permission.
A personal affair, without permission, within established administrative procedures.
A political or administrative nature.
Official secrets, subject to existing regulatory standards.
questions.explanation: No Officer shall, without the express permission of his Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office, speak in public or broadcast on any matter which may reasonably be regarded as of a political or administrative nature.

questions.question: Under the Performance Management System (PMS), performance expectations shall be based on the detailed contents captured in which document
options: The National Budget.
Job Descriptions (JDs).
The Leave Roster.
The Reward and Recognition Scheme (R&RS).
questions.explanation: Job Descriptions (JDs) shall be available for each job, and used to capture detailed job contents including scope, responsibilities, reporting lines, etc. Performance expectations shall be based on the contents of JD.

questions.question: The **PSR** includes emerging issues such as Performance Management, Talent Sourcing, Volunteerism, and what other ICT-related trend
options: Biometric Verification.
Virtual Meetings/Engagements.
Cloud Storage Policy.
Electronic Mail Only Policy.
questions.explanation: The 2021 Edition of Public Service Rules (PSR) includes emerging issues such as Performance Management System, Talent Sourcing, Volunteerism, Virtual Meetings/Engagements.

questions.question: When can an officer on leave be recalled by the Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office
options: Only on weekends.
If they are required to return before the expiration of their authorized leave.
If the officer is abroad.
Only if they are GL 07 and above.
questions.explanation: An Officer may be required to return to duty before the expiration of his authorized leave by the Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office.

questions.question: The promotion process for officers involves a selection process including but not limited to written examination, oral interview, and what other technical assessment
options: Financial audit.
Test of ICT proficiency.
Physical fitness test.
Psychological evaluation.
questions.explanation: Selection shall be through competitive examination process including but not limited to written examination, test of ICT proficiency and oral interview.

questions.question: When an Officer is promoted, when shall the payment of promotion arrears be made
options: After six months.
Within the year promotion is effected.
Upon retirement.
Immediately the letter is issued.
questions.explanation: Payment of promotion arrears shall be made within the year promotion is effected.

questions.question: What is the maximum tenure for a Director, subject to satisfactory performance, under the Tenure Policy
options: Four years renewable for a further four years.
Five years renewable.
Ten years non-renewable.
Six years total.
questions.explanation: A Director.. shall compulsively retire upon serving eight years on the post; and a Permanent Secretary shall hold office for a term of four years and renewable for a further term of four years. (The Director rule mentions 8 years, while the PS rule mentions 4+4 years).

questions.question: What is the minimum number of years an officer on **GL.07**–14 must spend in post before being considered eligible for promotion
options: 2 years.
3 years.
4 years.
5 years.
questions.explanation: The minimum number of years that an Officer must spend in a post before being considered eligible for promotion for GL.07–14 is Minimum of 3 years.

questions.question: What is the maximum period of continuous absence from duty without leave that makes an officer liable to dismissal (deemed to have resigned)
options: 7 days.
10 days.
14 days.
30 days.
questions.explanation: An Officer absent without Leave for 14 days renders himself liable to dismissal.

questions.question: Who must countersign the Certificate of Service issued to an officer holding senior posts (**GL 07** and above)
options: Operationally, the minister under normal controls.
The Permanent Secretary of the retiring Officer and the Permanent Secretary, Federal Civil Service Commission.
In practice, the director hrm only within authorized procedures.
Within public service systems, the accountant general in standard administrative workflow.
questions.explanation: Certificate of service to be issued to Officers holding senior posts on GL.07 and above, requires the countersignature of the Permanent Secretary of the retiring Officer and the Permanent Secretary, Federal Civil Service Commission.

questions.question: Which specific allowance is not payable if the transfer is at the Officer's own request
options: Duty Tour Allowance.
Kilometer Allowance.
Resettlement Allowance.
Shift Duty Allowance.
questions.explanation: An Officer whose transfer is at his own request shall be entitled to only transport allowance and not resettlement allowance.

questions.question: What is the rate of payment for **Resettlement Allowance**'
options: 5 per cent of annual emolument.
2 per cent of an Officer's annual total emolument.
10 per cent of monthly salary.
A fixed amount of N50,000.
questions.explanation: Resettlement Allowance shall be paid at the rate of 2 per cent of an Officer’s annual total emolument.

questions.question: Who shall be paid the **Non-accident bonus** at the rate of N50,000.00 per annum
options: All officers who drive government vehicles for any duty assignment.
Drivers with at least six months of accident-free service within the year.
All senior officers assigned official vehicles within approved transport pools.
Only drivers attached exclusively to ministers and heads of agencies.
questions.explanation: Non-accident bonus shall be paid at the rate of N50,000.00 per annum, for drivers who are not involved in an accident.. and have been driving for a period of not less than six months in a year.

questions.question: Which form of reward includes certificates, engraved plaques, medals, cash gifts, and sponsorship for attendance of Masterclass and local training programmes
options: Acting Allowance.
Resettlement Allowance.
Reward for Outstanding Work and Meritorious Service.
Duty Tour Allowance.
questions.explanation: Rewards for each of the awards shall be in the form of certificates, letter of recommendation, engraved plaques or trophies, medals, cash gifts, sponsorship..

questions.question: What is the primary objective of staff development policies
options: To promote only senior staff.
To enhance the knowledge, skills, attitude, effectiveness and efficiency of staff.
To reduce the workforce.
To replace permanent staff with contract staff.
questions.explanation: Objectives of Staff Development.. to enhance the knowledge, skills, attitude, effectiveness and efficiency of staff.

questions.question: Under the **Performance Management Cycle**, when shall individual performance planning be done
options: At the end of the year.
Mid-year.
At the beginning of the Performance Management Cycle.
Only when the officer requests it.
questions.explanation: Individual Performance Planning shall be done at the beginning of the Performance Management Cycle.

questions.question: Which **PSR** rule prohibits officers from borrowing money in any manner that places them under a financial obligation to a person within the Public Service
options: Rule 100427 (Seeking Influence).
Rule 100432 (Borrowing Money).
Rule 100416 (Unauthorized Disclosure).
Rule 100424 (Investments).
questions.explanation: No Officer may borrow money either as principal or as surety or in any manner which places him under a financial obligation to a person within the Public Service.

questions.question: What is the entitlement for **Annual Leave** for officers on **GL 04**–06
options: 14 working days.
21 working days.
30 working days.
45 calendar days.
questions.explanation: Annual Leave entitlement for GL 04–06 is 21 working days.

questions.question: What is the compensation for an officer who sustains an injury during official duties which incapacitates them, such that they are entitled to be discharged from sick leave or permanently invalided
options: Entitled to draw full emolument until he is discharged.
Procedurally, payment of half salary under applicable policy conditions.
In practice, immediate dismissal within authorized procedures.
Within public service systems, transfer to a desk job in standard administrative workflow.
questions.explanation: An Officer who is incapacitated as a result of injury sustained in the course of his official duties shall be entitled to draw full emolument until he is discharged from sick leave or permanently invalided.

questions.question: What does the **PSR** state must be done with all records generated during any of the phases of the annual Performance Management cycle
options: Operationally, destroyed immediately under normal controls.
Procedurally, used only for public consultation under applicable policy conditions.
Administered in line with good administrative document and data management practices.
Within public service systems, sent to the national assembly in standard administrative workflow.
questions.explanation: All records generated during any of the phases of the annual Performance Management cycle.. shall be administered in line with good administrative document and data management practices.

questions.question: select the statement that correctly defines the penalty for failure to submit demand representations regarding unsatisfactory conduct within the specified time limit?
options: Appropriate sanction will be invoked against the officer.
The representations are accepted late.
The officer is promoted.
The officer is transferred.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Appropriate sanction will be invoked against the officer.). Failure to submit them within this time limit will be taken to mean that you do not wish to make any, and appropriate sanction will be invoked against you.

questions.question: Which authority is responsible for the approval of conditions of service, schemes of service, and organizational structure for Parastatals
options: Supervising Minister.
The Board/Council.
The Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF).
Federal Ministry of Finance.
questions.explanation: Conditions of Service, Scheme of Service and the Organizational Structure for Parastatals shall be approved by the Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.

questions.question: What is the required qualification standard for **Interns and Volunteers**'
options: Operationally, primary school certificate only under normal controls.
Secondary School Certificate Examination (SSCE) or equivalent, up to University Degree/HND.
In practice, masters degree only within authorized procedures.
Within public service systems, only specialized professional certification in standard administrative workflow.
questions.explanation: Interns must be postgraduates, undergraduates, or students in other tertiary institutions while volunteers must possess a University Degree, HND, NCE, ND and SSCE or its equivalent.

questions.question: What is the minimum period of service required for an officer to qualify for Annual Leave in a given year
options: Operationally, less than six months under normal controls.
Not less than six months previous service within a leave earning service year.
In practice, one month within authorized procedures.
Within public service systems, twelve months in standard administrative workflow.
questions.explanation: An Officer shall qualify for Annual Leave not less than six months previous service within a leave earning service year.

questions.question: For a senior officer seeking a Certificate of Service (Form Gen. 58), whose countersignature is required in addition to the Permanent Secretary of the retiring officer
options: The Auditor General.
The Permanent Secretary, Federal Civil Service Commission.
The Minister of Labour.
The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
questions.explanation: Certificate of service to be issued to Officers holding senior posts on GL.07 and above, requires the countersignature of the Permanent Secretary of the retiring Officer and the Permanent Secretary, Federal Civil Service Commission.

questions.question: For effective public service & institutional reforms, what is the most appropriate approach to ensure public service & institutional reforms compliance
options: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.

Rely on credible official sources and confirm facts before conclusions.
Prioritize convenience over approved process requirements.
Apply discretionary shortcuts to accelerate closure regardless of controls.
questions.explanation: Rely on credible official sources and confirm facts before conclusions.

Proceed without validating source records and decision criteria.
Treat exceptions as normal practice without written justification.

Rely on credible official sources and confirm facts before conclusions.

Bypass review controls to save time.
Use verified official sources and state implications for public service.
Delay documentation until after implementation.

They are promoted to the next grade level.

Two years in the first instance, with extensions up to four years.

General knowledge and current affairs.
Laws and regulations governing their respective services.
Advanced management techniques. □.

questions.question: select the option that best answers: An Accounting Officer is in charge for ensuring that any revenue collected is:?

Spent immediately on ministry needs.
Remitted to the appropriate authorities promptly and not spent.
Used to cover immediate operational expenses.

Six months.

The Bureau for Monitoring and Price Intelligence Unit (BMPIU)

30 days
21 daysa.

To be the Accounting Officer of the Parastatal.
To guarantee the Parastatal's policies align with government policies.
To manage the day-to-day operations of the Parastatal.

questions.question: According to established rules, select the option that best answers: Which reform introduced the concept that Permanent Secretaries were no longer the Accounting Officers of Ministries?

The Civil Service Handbook of 2009.3.
The 1988 Civil Service Reforms.
The Udoji Public Service Review Commission of 1972.

To allow all intermediate officers to be acquainted with the decisions reached by the higher authority.

At the end of the calendar year.
After three months.

questions.question: The Public Service Rules (**PSR**) apply to all officers except where specific terms in a contract or letter of appointment conflict with them. What must the **PSR** itself conform to
options: Federal Ministry of Finance Guidelines.
The Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria.
Extant Circulars issued by the OHCSF.
The Nigerian Foreign Service Regulations.
questions.explanation: The Rules apply only to the extent that they are not inconsistent with the provisions of the Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria.

questions.question: In the **PSR** rule numbering system, what do the first two digits indicate
options: The Rule Number.
The Section Number.
The Chapter Number.
The Year of Amendment.
questions.explanation: Each Rule has been given a number containing six digits. The first two indicate the Chapter.

questions.question: Which body holds the authority for making appointments to posts graded **GL.07**–17 in the Federal Civil Service
options: Permanent Secretaries of the respective MDAs.
The Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF).
The Federal Civil Service Commission (FCSC).
The Junior Staff Committee (JSC).
questions.explanation: The Federal Civil Service Commission shall make appointments to posts graded GL.07-17.

questions.question: What is the consequence for an officer who changes their date of birth recorded upon appointment throughout their career
options: Promotion deferment.
Immediate retirement.
Act of serious misconduct.
Warning letter only.
questions.explanation: Date of birth recorded on appointment by an officer shall not be changed throughout the career of the Officer. Any contravention shall be regarded as an act of serious misconduct.

questions.question: What is the deadline for the submission of applications from interested candidates following a recruitment advertisement by the **FCSC**'
options: Two weeks.
One month.
Six weeks.
Three months.
questions.explanation: The deadline for submission of applications from interested candidates shall be six weeks from the date of placement of the advertisement.

questions.question: Which officer category is handled by the Junior Staff Committee (JSC) of each Ministry/Office regarding appointments
options: Senior Posts (GL 07 and above).
Junior Staff on GL. 06 and below.
Contract Staff (SGL 12 and above).
Permanent Secretaries.
questions.explanation: Each Ministry/Extra-Ministerial Office shall appoint Junior Staff on GL. 06 and below. This shall be handled by the Junior Staff Committee.

questions.question: What is the policy regarding the employment of unpaid staff in the Federal Public Service
options: It is encouraged for internship programs.
It is restricted to GL 01-03.
It is prohibited.
It requires ministerial approval.
questions.explanation: The employment of unpaid Staff is prohibited.

questions.question: What standard rule applies when an Officer is promoted to a grade level that does not overlap their old grade level
options: They are placed on the next point higher than the former emolument.
They will be placed at the minimum point of their new salary grade level.
Their increment is deferred for one year.
They are required to take a competitive examination.
questions.explanation: If the Officer is promoted to a grade level that does not overlap his old grade level, he will be placed at the minimum point of his new salary grade level.

questions.question: If an increment is deferred or withheld, can it be restored with retrospective effect in consequence of improved service during a later period
options: Yes, if approved by the FCSC.
Yes, immediately.
No, increment is not restored retrospectively.
Only for officers on GL 15 and above.
questions.explanation: An increment deferred or withheld cannot be restored with retrospective effect in consequence of improved service during a later increment earning period [10-12].

questions.question: What term defines the temporary removal of an officer from performing his normal duties when disciplinary proceedings for dismissal are undertaken, and the officer is paid half salary
options: Suspension.
Demotion.
Interdiction.
Termination.
questions.explanation: Interdiction means the temporary removal of an officer.. during which he shall be placed on half salary.

questions.question: Who must approve the deployment of an officer to a duty station or department, referred to as 'Posting'
options: The Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office.
The Director HR.
The FCSC.
The Staff Union.
questions.explanation: Posting is the initial assignment of an officer to position (job schedule). The Director HR submits reports on posting instructions.

questions.question: The **PSR** strongly advises that the posting system should not be used as a tool for:
options: Acquiring whole-service experience.
Coercion, punishment, or cronyism.
Filling critical gaps in specialized skills.
Promoting high-priority service-wide projects.
questions.explanation: Fairness to all Officers shall be an underlying principle in order to ensure that the system is not used as a tool for coercion, punishment or cronyism.

questions.question: An officer on an official assignment is entitled to airfare, depending on the exigencies and with the approval of whom
options: The Permanent Secretary, CMO.
The President.
The Accounting Officer.
The Director of Finance.
questions.explanation: All Officers on official assignment are entitled to airfare, depending on the exigencies and with the approval of the Accounting Officer.

questions.question: Which allowance is payable to officers traveling to towns and cities where air transport services do not exist, based on distance
options: Duty Tour Allowance (DTA).
Estacode Allowance.
Kilometer Allowance.
Transport and Local Running Allowance.
questions.explanation: All Officers when travelling to towns and cities where air transport services do not exist shall be paid Kilometer Allowance.

questions.question: How much of their Duty Tour Allowance (DTA) are officers granted for the purpose of local running (transport and local errands)
options: 10% of DTA.
20% of DTA.
30% of DTA.
50% of DTA.
questions.explanation: For the purpose of local running, Officers shall be granted 30% of their duty tour allowance in addition to airport taxi, at the prevailing rates.

questions.question: Who must provide security guidelines for all high-level intra and inter-governmental virtual meetings
options: The Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF).
The Federal Ministry of Communications and Digital Economy.
The Federal Civil Service Commission (FCSC).
The National Assembly.
questions.explanation: Security of Government data/information.. shall be conducted in line with the Guidelines prescribed from time to time by the Federal Ministry of Communications and Digital Economy.

questions.question: What is strictly prohibited for participants during virtual meetings, especially high-level engagements
options: Use of mute technology.
Sidebar conversations and multi-tasking.
Observing short breaks.
Identifying themselves when they speak.
questions.explanation: No sidebar conversations and No multi-tasking (e.g., running e-mail or treating files while participating in virtual meetings).

questions.question: Which category of allowances is NOT explicitly listed in Chapter 14 of the **PSR**'
options: Warm Clothing Allowance.
Books Allowance.
Paternity Allowance.
Kilometer Allowance.
questions.explanation: Paternity Allowance is not explicitly listed in Rule 140102, which lists Acting, Books, Call Duty, Disengagement, Duty Tour, Estacode, Hazard, Kilometer, Overtime, Resettlement, etc. (Paternity Leave is a time entitlement, not a cash allowance listed here).

questions.question: Within government administration, which option correctly addresses: What must officers do with historical manuscripts or other documents of public interest discovered in the course of their official duties?
options: Sell them to private collectors, in line with formal public service practice.
Report their existence to the Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra Ministerial Office for preservation.
Publish them immediately, subject to existing regulatory standards.
Appropriate them for their own use, under the applicable service framework.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Report their existence to the Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra Ministerial Office for preservation.). Historical manuscripts.. must not be appropriated to his own use, but their existence must be reported to Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra Ministerial Office in order that steps may be taken for their examination and preservation.

questions.question: Which authority is delegated full disciplinary powers over officers on Salary **GL. 13** and below, with the exception of dismissal
options: The FCSC.
The Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
Permanent Secretaries and Heads of Extra-Ministerial Offices.
The Minister.
questions.explanation: The Federal Civil Service Commission has delegated full disciplinary powers to Permanent Secretaries and Heads of Extra-Ministerial Offices in respect of officers on Salary G.L. 13 and below with the exception of the power of dismissal which has been delegated only from G.L. 06 and below.

questions.question: What is the consequence of abstracting or copying official documents without special permission
options: Formal reprimand issued for first breach under established records protocol.
It is prohibited and may attract disciplinary action where prior approval was not obtained.
Minor misconduct finding with caution where no classified material is involved.
Administrative redeployment pending review of records management compliance.
questions.explanation: Every Officer is prohibited from abstracting or copying official minutes, records or other documents except in accordance with official routine or with special permission of his Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office.

questions.question: An appeal made by an aggrieved officer for reconsideration of a decision must be handled and concluded within what maximum period
options: 90 days.
One year.
Six months.
18 months.
questions.explanation: An appeal from an aggrieved Officer shall be handled and concluded within six months.

questions.question: What is the penalty for an officer who accepts a paid appointment during leave of absence without prior approval
options: The officer shall not accept paid employment without the express permission of the Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF).
Procedurally, warning, within established administrative procedures under applicable policy conditions.
Payment of a fine, in line with formal public service practice.
Within formal administration, withheld increment, subject to existing regulatory standards under due process conditions.
questions.explanation: An Officer on leave of absence shall not accept any paid employment without previously obtaining the express approval of the Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF).

questions.question: What rule applies to an officer who finds valuable minerals or makes a discovery during their official duties
options: They must give 10% to the Government.
They must give full information to Government under pain of dismissal.
They may sell the information privately, in line with formal public service practice.
They must keep it a secret, subject to existing regulatory standards.
questions.explanation: It is the duty of every Officer to report to Government any discovery of what he considers to be valuable minerals. Officers are prohibited, under pain of dismissal, from giving any information on such valuable discoveries to any syndicate, company or individual.

questions.question: What is the definition of **Suspension** in the **PSR**'
options: Removal of an Officer from performing the duties of his office, with half salary.
Temporary removal from office, with full salary, within established administrative procedures.
Removal of an Officer from performing the duties of his office.
A temporary transfer to a non-sensitive post, subject to existing regulatory standards.
questions.explanation: Suspension means removal of an Officer from performing the duties of his office.. during which period he shall earn no salary.

questions.question: What are **Senior Posts** defined as in the **PSR**'
options: Posts attracting emoluments on Grade Level GL 06 and below.
Posts attracting emoluments on Grade Level GL 07 and above.
All posts requiring a university degree.
Directorate Cadres only.
questions.explanation: Senior Posts are posts attracting emoluments on Grade Level GL.07 and above.

questions.question: What is the basis upon which an officer is considered for an award or recognition under the Reward and Recognition Scheme (R&RS)
options: Seniority only.
Political connection.
Outstanding performance of duties and exemplary conduct.
Attaining retirement age.
questions.explanation: The recipient of any of the awards must have been considered as the best on the basis of outstanding performance of duties and exemplary conduct as outlined in approved Rewards and Recognition Scheme (R&RS).

questions.question: Which body is responsible for ensuring the successful implementation of the Performance Management System (PMS) across all levels of officers in a Federal Public Service Institution
options: The FCSC.
The Accounting Officer.
The Director HRM.
The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF).
questions.explanation: The Accounting Officer of every Federal Public Service Institution shall be responsible for ensuring the successful implementation of the PMS across all levels of officers.

questions.question: The final Progress Report rendered on an officer after 21 months' service shall include a definite recommendation on which of the following outcomes
options: Transfer or secondment only.
Whether the appointment should be confirmed, terminated, or the contract renewed.
Promotion to the next grade level.
Granting of Sabbatical Leave.
questions.explanation: The final Progress Report rendered after 21 months' service shall include a definite recommendation as to whether the Officer's appointment should be confirmed or terminated or whether his contract should be renewed.

questions.question: According to established rules, at what point an Officer's increment has been withheld, the **FCSC** may grant one or more **special increments** at a subsequent incremental date to mitigate what?
options: The cost of living.
The administrative fees.
The officer’s poor performance.
The lasting effects of the withheld increment.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (The lasting effects of the withheld increment.). The FCSC may at a subsequent incremental date grant one or more special increments having the effects of raising the salary of the Officer to or towards the level it would have reached if the previous withholding has not occurred.

questions.question: which option correctly addresses: All decisions on deferment or withholding of increment and stoppage of salaries must be communicated to the officer concerned within what period?
options: Three months.
Two weeks.
Six weeks.
One month.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Two weeks.). All decisions on deferment or withholding of increment and stoppage of salaries must be communicated.. to the Officer concerned within two weeks.

questions.question: Under the **Official Secrets Act**, what is the definition of 'classified matter'
options: Any information concerning foreign policy, under the applicable service framework.
Procedurally, information or thing which under applicable policy conditions.
Personal files of senior officers, in line with formal public service practice.
Official minutes of board meetings, subject to existing regulatory standards.
questions.explanation: ‘Classified matter’ means any information or thing which, under any system of security classification, is not to be disclosed to the public and of which the disclosure to the public would be prejudicial to the security of Nigeria.

questions.question: What is the effect on promotion eligibility for officers who are currently falling within the field of selection but are under a disciplinary action
options: They are automatically promoted.
They shall be considered eligible after the conclusion of the disciplinary action.
They shall be considered except those who are under disciplinary action.
Their selection is deferred until the next cycle.
questions.explanation: All Officers who fall within the field of selection for any promotion exercise shall be considered except those who are under disciplinary action.

questions.question: In assessing the merit of an officer for promotion, what records are taken into account, especially when candidates have equal potential
options: Political affiliations.
Senority and previous records of performance.
Marital status.
Grade Level of their spouse.
questions.explanation: Seniority and previous records of performance will be taken into account in choosing between candidates with equal potential for promotion.

questions.question: Who must approve the Conditions of Service, Scheme of Service, and Organizational Structure for Parastatals
options: The Parastatal Board/Council alone.
The Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF).
The Federal Civil Service Commission (FCSC).
The Supervising Ministry.
questions.explanation: Conditions of Service, Scheme of Service and the Organizational Structure for Parastatals shall be approved by the Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation, on the recommendation of the various Boards/ Councils.

questions.question: What is the ultimate penalty for **Serious Misconduct**'
options: Withholding of increment.
Transfer.
Dismissal.
Reprimand.
questions.explanation: The ultimate penalty for serious misconduct is dismissal.

questions.question: In cases of dismissal, what is forfeited by the officer, according to the **PSR**'
options: Only their salary for that month.
All claims to retiring benefits, leave or transport grant.
Only their house.
Half of their pension.
questions.explanation: The ultimate penalty for serious misconduct is dismissal. An Officer who is dismissed forfeits all claims to retiring benefits, leave or transport grant etc.

questions.question: In Parastatals, which committee handles appointments and discipline of officers on **GL 06** and below
options: Senior Staff Committee (SSC).
Junior Staff Committee (JSC).
Chief Executive Officer (CEO) only.
Board/Council.

questions.question: Within government administration, which role is enjoined to acquaint themselves with these Regulations, the Finance (Control and Management) Act, and relevant Sections of the Constitution dealing with public finance?
options: Public Officers.
Only External Auditors. □
Only Ministers.
Only the Accountant-General.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Public Officers.). The Preface states: "Public Officers, are enjoined to acquaint themselves with these Regulations, the Finance (Control and Management) Act, Cap. 144 Laws of the federation of Nigeria 1990, as well as the relevant Sections of the Constitution dealing with public finance, for proper guidance."
questions.chapter: National Governance & Policy Developments - Expansion Set

questions.question: responsibility for is accountable for maintaining a payment voucher register and confirming the accuracy of entries before signing a voucher belongs to which role?
options: The Head of Finance and Accounts.
The Officer controlling the vote.
The Internal Auditor
The Sub-Accounting Officer.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (The Officer controlling the vote.). Financial Regulation 605 states: "Officer controlling vote must keep a disbursement voucher register... Under no circumstances must he sign a voucher until it has been entered in the register and has accordingly confirmed the accuracy of the entries therein."
questions.chapter: National Governance & Policy Developments - Expansion Set

subcategories.name: International & Regional Affairs
subcategories.description: Regional and global affairs relevant to Nigeria.

questions.question: An Officer traveling abroad for a short period is eligible for which allowance
options: Duty Tour Allowance (DTA).
Resettlement Allowance.
Estacode Allowance.
Local Course Allowance.
questions.explanation: Officers shall be eligible for appropriate estacode allowance at the prevailing rates when they undertake special duty abroad or are on a delegation or visit to an organization abroad for a short period.

questions.question: If an officer is required to proceed to a foreign country on duty or an approved course of instruction in a cold or temperate climate, they are entitled to which allowance
options: Estacode Supplementation Allowance.
Warm Clothing Allowance.
Local Running Allowance.
Project Allowance.
questions.explanation: An Officer who is required to proceed to a foreign country on duty or on an approved course of instruction will be eligible for a warm clothing allowance.

questions.question: If the donor providing external training also provides free boarding and lodging, the officer concerned shall be entitled to what percentage of their appropriate estacode for the whole duration of the course
options: 10 per cent.
25 per cent.
40 per cent.
100 per cent.
questions.explanation: Where the donor providing the training as a form of technical assistance to Nigeria also provides free boarding and lodging, the Officer concerned shall be entitled to 10 per cent of his appropriate estacode for the whole duration of the course.

questions.question: What is the compensation benefit for an officer in the Foreign Service who sustains injury during official duties, resulting in incapacitation and permanent invalidation
options: Entitled to draw full emolument until he is discharged from sick leave or permanently invalided.
Procedurally, promotion, within established administrative procedures under applicable policy conditions.
Immediate dismissal, in line with formal public service practice.
Pension payment only, subject to existing regulatory standards.
questions.explanation: An Officer who is incapacitated as a result of injury sustained in the course of his official duties shall be entitled to draw full emolument until he is discharged from sick leave or permanently invalided.

questions.question: Where an officer is on an official assignment outside Nigeria, what is the entitlement for the officer's spouse regarding air passage
options: Free air passage only if the duration of duty is not less than 9 months.
Free air passage regardless of duration, within established administrative procedures.
Half fare only, in line with formal public service practice.
Not eligible for passage, subject to existing regulatory standards.
questions.explanation: Air passage for himself only, but where the duration of the Officer's duties is not less than 9 months, his spouse may accompany him at Government expense.

questions.question: What is prohibited concerning domestic staff of an entitled Officer at an overseas post, according to Foreign Service Regulations
options: Paying their social security contribution.
Employing a Nigerian.
Conversion of domestic staff to locally recruited staff.
Granting them leave.
questions.explanation: Conversion of domestic staff of an entitled Officer shall not be converted to locally recruited staff.

questions.question: Who must approve an officer's official visit while on leave outside Nigeria
options: The Minister of Foreign Affairs.
Prior approval of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
The President.
The FCSC.
questions.explanation: Before an Officer on leave outside Nigeria undertakes official visit, prior approval of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation shall be obtained.

questions.question: What is the entitlement for a Head of Diplomatic Mission regarding domestic staff
options: One cook and two security guards, under the applicable service framework.
One cook and two stewards; two security guards; and two drivers.
Three servants only, in line with formal public service practice.
Local staff only, subject to existing regulatory standards.
questions.explanation: The Head of Mission shall be entitled to the following domestic staff: one cook and two stewards; two security guards; and two drivers.

questions.question: The contribution of an Officer to secondary education of each child at an overseas post is what percentage of the cost (boarding and tuition inclusive)
options: 10 per cent.
15 per cent.
20 per cent.
25 per cent.
questions.explanation: The contribution of an Officer to secondary education in respect of each child shall be 15 per cent of the cost (boarding and tuition inclusive).

questions.question: An officer serving in an overseas post may spend up to what fraction of his annual leave entitlement overseas
options: One quarter.
One half.
Two thirds.
The full entitlement.
questions.explanation: An Officer serving in an overseas post may, subject to the exigencies of the Service, be permitted within any calendar year to spend up to half of his annual leave entitlement for that year as casual leave overseas.

questions.question: Which protocol is used for secure data transmission over the web
options: HTTP.
FTP.
HTTPS.
SMTP.
questions.explanation: HTTPS uses SSL/TLS to encrypt web traffic [111-113].

questions.question: The second-largest continent in the world is:
options: Asia.
Africa.
Europe.
North America.
questions.explanation: Africa is the second largest continent in the world (cited in previous current affairs discussion). [Source not present in new excerpts, relying on established conversational fact].

questions.question: Which allowance covers lodging and per diem for officers on duty outside Nigeria
options: DTA.
Kilometer Allowance.
Estacode Allowance.
Hardship Allowance.
questions.explanation: Estacode Allowance is eligible for officers undertaking special duty or courses abroad.

questions.question: When an officer is seconded to the service of an approved international organization, what is this period defined as
options: Permanent transfer.
Temporary release for a specified period.
Resignation.
Casual leave.
questions.explanation: Secondment means the temporary release of an Officer to the service of another government approved body or any recognized International Organization or body for a specified period.

questions.question: An Officer who is on Leave of Absence on grounds of Public Policy (e.g., to join a spouse abroad) is granted what status regarding their service continuity
options: Break in Service.
Leave without pay.
Dismissal.
Transfer.
questions.explanation: Leave of Absence on grounds of Public Policy may be granted to a Foreign Service Officer who is also a Public Officer, to enable him join his spouse on posting abroad, granted as leave without pay.
questions.chapter: Leave on Grounds of Public Policy

questions.question: The two branches of the Nigerian Foreign Service are Branch A (Diplomatic) and Branch B (Non-Diplomatic). Officers who maintain proficiency in a language below standard are encouraged to do what
options: Transfer to Nigeria.
Take language lessons at Government expense.
Retire immediately.
Seek private employment.
questions.explanation: Officers who maintain proficiency in language lower standard or higher standard shall qualify for a one-off payment of 5 percent or 10 percent of Consolidated Foreign Service Allowance respectively.

questions.question: Who provides the detailed instructions and allowances regarding overseas duty or course of instruction
options: The Officer's Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office.
The FCSC, within established administrative procedures.
The Minister of Education, in line with formal public service practice.
The Ministry of Finance, subject to existing regulatory standards.
questions.explanation: An Officer required to undertake duty or a course of instruction outside Nigeria must be furnished with detailed instructions regarding such duties or course and be informed in advance and in writing of the allowances and traveling facilities for which he will be eligible for at Government expense; by his Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office.

questions.question: If an officer serving overseas requires medical treatment that exceeds Chapter 13 requirements, what condition must be satisfied for Government to bear the expense
options: The officer must pay 50% of the cost.
The officer must obtain prior medical certification that they urgently need treatment outside Nigeria.
The officer must be GL 17, in line with formal public service practice.
The officer must have served 20 years.
questions.explanation: Government will bear the expenses.. provided that a Nigerian Officer urgently needs a specialist attention outside that country, certified by the Head of Mission based on medical opinion.

questions.question: When was the FIFA World Cup hosted by Qatar
questions.explanation: Qatar hosted the FIFA World Cup in 2022.

questions.question: For sea travel, what is the maximum container size entitlement for an Officer on SGL. 13 and above
options: 20-foot container.
40-foot container.
50-foot container.
10-foot container.
questions.explanation: Officer on SGL. 13 and above shall be entitled to a maximum of 40 foot container.

questions.question: The **PSR** mandates that all officers must be placed on the **IPPIS** platform within what period of assumption of duty
options: One week.
Two months.
Six months.
One year.
questions.explanation: On first appointment, emolument, shall, as a general rule be paid as from the date of assumption of duty. All Officers shall be placed on IPPIS platform within two months of assumption of duty.

questions.question: Who must approve the deployment of staff in the Nigerian Civil Service
options: The Minister of Communications.
The Federal Civil Service Commission.
The Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF).
The National Assembly.
questions.explanation: Appointments to offices in the Federal Civil Service are made on the authority of the Federal Civil Service Commission. Permanent Secretaries/Heads of Extra-Ministerial Offices are authorized to appoint candidates where powers have been delegated.

questions.question: An officer on duty visit outside Nigeria is eligible to receive Estacode Allowance. What additional allocation is given if the officer incurs delegation or entertainment expense abroad
options: No additional allowance.
A fixed $100 bonus.
A special allocation authorized for that purpose.
Only reimbursement of actual expenses.
questions.explanation: When it seems likely that a delegation or any individual Officer going abroad will have to incur expenditure on entertainment, a special allocation will be authorized for that purpose.

questions.question: The **FCSC** makes appointments to posts graded **GL.07**–17. The deadline for application submissions is six weeks from the date of advertisement placement in how many national newspapers
options: Two.
Three.
Four.
Five.
questions.explanation: Advertisement shall be in three national newspapers and the Commission's website.

questions.question: An officer who is injured in the course of his official duties and is permanently invalided will retire in accordance with which rule
options: Rule 120244 (Leave on Permanent Invalidation).
Rule 100407 (Dismissal).
Rule 020904 (Resignation).
Rule 020908 (Mandatory Retirement).
questions.explanation: An Officer who is recommended by a Medical Board or a Healthcare Provider to be permanently invalided will forthwith commence vacation leave prior to retirement in accordance with Rule 120244.

questions.question: Which allowance is granted to enable Officers pay for lodging and feeding expenses during approved official tours within Nigeria
options: Estacode Allowance.
Duty Tour Allowance (DTA).
Resettlement Allowance.
Warm Clothing Allowance.
questions.explanation: Duty Tour Allowance is granted to enable Officers pay for lodging and feeding expenses during approved official tours.

questions.question: What is the penalty if an officer fails to comply with instructions delivered to him at his address within seven days of being placed on Interdiction
options: Warning.
Payment of a fine.
Deemed as absent from duty without leave.
Temporary transfer.
questions.explanation: If he fails to comply with the instructions delivered to him at such address within seven days of such delivery, he will be regarded as absent from duty without leave.

questions.question: An officer proceeding or returning from an overseas posting is entitled to free air passage or sea passage for himself, his wife, and how many children
options: Two children.
Three children.
Four children.
No children.
questions.explanation: An Officer proceeding or returning from an overseas posting.. is eligible for free air passage or sea passage as laid down in the Public Service Rules or extant Circulars for self, wife and four children.

questions.question: What is the compensation for an officer in the Foreign Service who sustains injury during official duties which incapacitates them, such that they are entitled to be discharged from sick leave or permanently invalided
options: Entitled to draw full emolument until he is discharged.
Payment of half salary, within established administrative procedures.
Immediate dismissal, in line with formal public service practice.
Pension payment only, subject to existing regulatory standards.
questions.explanation: An Officer who is incapacitated as a result of injury sustained in the course of his official duties shall be entitled to draw full emolument until he is discharged from sick leave or permanently invalided.

questions.question: Which of the following bodies is NOT listed as a stakeholder for the National Council on Establishments (NCE)
options: National Assembly Service Commission.
Federal Civil Service Commission.
National Judicial Service Commission.
Federal Ministry of Information.
questions.explanation: NCE stakeholders include FCSC, National Assembly Service Commission, and National Judicial Service Commission, but not Federal Ministry of Information.

questions.question: The National Council on Establishments (NCE) was established in what year
questions.explanation: The National Council on Establishment was established in 1957.

questions.question: What is the core function of the National Council on Establishments (NCE)
options: Approving the national budget, under the applicable service framework.
Approving the appointment of Ministers, within established administrative procedures.
Ensuring periodic review/update of the Schemes of Service and approving new cadres.
Overseeing discipline of all public officers, subject to existing regulatory standards.
questions.explanation: Functions of the National Council on Establishment: ensures periodic review/update of the Schemes of Service; approves new cadres..

questions.question: Who provides security guidelines for virtual meetings in line with **Rule 090104**'
options: The Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF).
The Federal Ministry of Communications and Digital Economy.
The Federal Civil Service Commission (FCSC).
The National Assembly.
questions.explanation: Security of Government data/information.. shall be conducted in line with the Guidelines prescribed from time to time by the Federal Ministry of Communications and Digital Economy.

questions.question: What is the penalty for failure to submit required representations regarding unsatisfactory conduct within the specified time limit
options: The representations are accepted late.
Appropriate sanction will be invoked against the officer.
The officer is transferred.
The officer is promoted.
questions.explanation: Failure to submit them within this time limit will be taken to mean that you do not wish to make any, and appropriate sanction will be invoked against you.

questions.question: What must officers do with historical manuscripts or other documents of public interest discovered in the course of their official duties
options: Appropriate them for their own use, under the applicable service framework.
Report their existence to the Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra Ministerial Office for preservation.
Sell them to private collectors, in line with formal public service practice.
Publish them immediately, subject to existing regulatory standards.
questions.explanation: Historical manuscripts.. shall not be appropriated to his own use, but their existence must be reported to Permanent Secretary/ Head of Extra Ministerial Office in order that steps may be taken for their examination and preservation.

questions.question: When an Officer's increment has been withheld, the **FCSC** may grant one or more **special increments** at a subsequent incremental date to mitigate what
options: The cost of living.
The lasting effects of the withheld increment.
The officer’s poor performance.
The administrative fees.
questions.explanation: The FCSC may at a subsequent incremental date grant one or more special increments having the effects of raising the salary of the Officer to or towards the level it would have reached if the previous withholding has not occurred.

questions.question: All decisions on deferment or withholding of increment and stoppage of salaries must be communicated to the officer concerned within what period
options: Two weeks.
One month.
Six weeks.
Three months.
questions.explanation: All decisions on deferment or withholding of increment and stoppage of salaries shall be communicated.. to the Officer concerned within two weeks.

ca_general.explanation: Correct option: B (The Accountant-General.). Financial Regulation 303 states: "The original copy of a Provisional General Warrant is addressed to the Accountant-General with a signed copy forwarded to the Auditor-General."

ca_general.question: Within government administration, which option correctly addresses: To whom should applications for supplementary provision be submitted?
options: To the Minister of Finance.
To the Auditor-General.
To the Head of Civil Service
Only to the Accountant-General.
ca_general.explanation: Correct option: A (To the Minister of Finance.). Financial Regulation 315 states: "applications for supplementary provision should be submitted to the Minister of Finance..."

ca_general.question: In the public service context, responsibility for has full authority to direct on all matters relating to the finance and accounting affairs of the federation, not assigned by law to any other Minister belongs to which role?
options: The Head of Civil Service
The Accountant-General.
The Minister of Finance.
The Auditor-General.
ca_general.explanation: Correct option: C (The Minister of Finance.). Financial Regulation 501 states: "By the provisions of Sections 3 and 4 of the Finance (Control and Management) Act Cap 144 Laws of the Federation, 1990, the Minister of Finance must have full authority to direct on all matters relating to the Finance and accounting affairs of the federation which are not by law assigned to any: other Minister."

ca_general.question: According to established rules, choose the best answer for: Before procuring safes, strong-rooms, cash tanks, and boxes, whose specification must first be obtained?
options: The Accountant-General.
The Auditor-General.a.
The Ministry of Finance.
The Head of Civil Service.
ca_general.explanation: Correct option: A (The Accountant-General.). Financial Regulation 1106(ii) necessitate that "their specification must first be obtained from the Accountant-General before procurement." 233

ca_general.question: which option correctly addresses: How often should annual returns of all Government strong-rooms and safes be rendered, and to whom?
options: Bi-annually, to the Head of Civil Service.a.
Annually on March 31st, to the Accountant-General.
Monthly, to the Auditor-General.
Quarterly, to the Minister of Finance.
ca_general.explanation: Correct option: B (Annually on March 31st, to the Accountant-General.). Financial Regulation 1115 necessitate "Annual returns in duplicate, of all Government strong-rooms and safes will be rendered on the 31st March by all ministries and extra-ministerial offices and other arms of government to the Accountant-General."

ca_general.question: which role is accountable for the general control of all Treasury and other receipt and licence books?
options: The Minister of Finance.
The Head of Civil Service. Q.
The Accountant-General.
The Auditor-General.
ca_general.explanation: Correct option: C (The Accountant-General.). Financial Regulation 1201 states that the Accountant-General is in charge for the general control of all Treasury and other receipt and licence books.

ca_general.question: In official practice, select the statement that correctly defines the procedure for dealing with any defects found in receipt or licence books upon receipt from the Federal Government Printer?
options: The books should be used as is.
The defects must be reported immediately to the Accountant-General and the Auditor-General.
The defects should be noted for future reference. □. 240
The books should be immediately returned to the printer.
ca_general.explanation: Correct option: B (The defects must be reported immediately to the Accountant-General and the Auditor-General.). Financial Regulation 1204 mandates immediate reporting of defects to the Accountant-General and Auditor-General.

ca_general.question: In official practice, which option aligns with the meaning of the disposition of triplicate copies of receipts?
options: They are forwarded to the Auditor-General.
They are retained in the receipt book.
They are destroyed.
They are returned to the Federal Government Printer. □.
ca_general.explanation: Correct option: B (They are retained in the receipt book.). Financial Regulation 1219 states that "triplicate copies of receipts must be retained in the receipt book." 245

ca_general.question: According to established rules, choose the best answer for: What does the Bureau do to guarantee compliance with the principles of due procedure?
options: It holds regular meetings with the private sector.
It prosecutes all cases of contract inflation.a.
It lobbies the National Assembly for more funding.
It reviews and certifies Federal Government contracts.
ca_general.explanation: Correct option: D (It reviews and certifies Federal Government contracts.). The Bureau reviews and certifies Federal Government contracts according to established thresholds to secure compliance with due procedure principles.

ca_general.question: According to established rules, which option correctly addresses: Which document is the main basis for the scrutiny of government accounts by the Public Accounts Committees?
options: The Finance (Control and Management) Act
The Annual Report of the Auditor-General for the Federation
The Civil Service Handbooka.
The Appropriation Act
ca_general.explanation: Correct option: B (The Annual Report of the Auditor-General for the Federation). The committees rely on the annual report of the Auditor-General for the Federation to scrutinize the accounts of MDAs.

ca_general.question: In official practice, select the option that best answers: What was the key objective of the Public Service Reform Programme of 1999? 408?
options: To increase the number of political appointees.
To introduce a new salary structure.3.
To reduce the size of the Civil Service.
To create a modern, professional, and efficient Civil Service.
ca_general.explanation: Correct option: D (To create a modern, professional, and efficient Civil Service.). The Public Service Reform Programme of 1999 was initiated to reposition the Civil Service to be a modern, professional, and efficient institution.

ca_general.question: select the statement that correctly defines described as a major cause for all the reform efforts in the Civil Service over the years?
options: The assiduous efforts of the government to overcome HRM challenges.
To create more government jobs.
To reduce staff salaries.a.
To increase political patronage.
ca_general.explanation: Correct option: A (The assiduous efforts of the government to overcome HRM challenges.). The government's assiduous efforts to overcome these challenges have been the major reason for all the reform efforts in the Civil Service over the years.

ca_general.question: In the public service context, which option best describes the Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF) in charge for?
options: General supervision of the affairs of the Civil Service.
Disciplinary control of all civil servants.
Auditing all government accounts.a.
Recruitment of junior staff.
ca_general.explanation: Correct option: A (General supervision of the affairs of the Civil Service.). The OHCSF provides general supervision of the affairs of the Civil Service.

ca_general.question: Within government administration, which office , in the end, pays the salaries of Civil Servants?
options: The President.
The government.
The Central Bank of Nigeria.a.. 438
The Nigerian tax payers.
ca_general.explanation: Correct option: D (The Nigerian tax payers.). Civil Servants must strive to offer quality service without let or hindrance at all times to the Nigerian tax payers who are, afterall, the ones who payment their salaries.

ca_general.question: In the public service context, choose the option that bodies is not a "paramilitary service" in the context of this section?
options: Nigeria Prison Service
Federal Ministry of Finance3
Nigeria Customs Service
Nigeria Immigration Service
ca_general.explanation: Correct option: B (Federal Ministry of Finance3). The Federal Ministry of Finance is a civil service ministry, not a paramilitary service.

ca_general.question: Within government administration, select the statement that correctly defines the principal source of provisions for government financial business in Nigeria?
options: The 1999 Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria. 3
The Annual Appropriation Act.
The Finance (Control and Management) Act.
Treasury Circulars. Q
ca_general.explanation: Correct option: A (The 1999 Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria. 3). Financial Regulation 102 states, "The 1999 Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria makes ample provisions for government financial business."

ca_general.question: which option correctly addresses: Where must each ministry, extra-ministerial office, and other arms of government sustain a separate bank account for revenue?
options: In a private trust account □
At the Central Bank of Nigeria or any other designated bank
In the ministry's vault
In a commercial bank of their choice
ca_general.explanation: Correct option: B (At the Central Bank of Nigeria or any other designated bank). Financial Regulation 213(i) mandates a separate bank account for Revenue at the Central Bank of Nigeria or any other bank designated by the Accountant-General.
